content.problemStatement:
Today chef has decided to cook a string **S** of length **N**. But he will not just cook the whole string, but a unique substring of the given string **S**. A substring is unique if it occurs in the string **S** only once.  Chef also does not like all the indices of a string. He wants a special index **i** to be in the unique substring. But the length of the unique substring should be minimized.

Chef wants your help to find an unique substring of minimum possible length which contains the index **i**. If there are several such substrings, chef will take the lexicographically smallest one. But chef has not told you the value of **i** yet. So you have to find the answer for all **i** (**1** to **N**).
constraints: S contains only lower case letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single string S of length N.
content.outputFormat: For each character in the string, print two space-separated integers Pi and Li, where Pi is the starting position (1-indexed) and Li is the length of the shortest unique substring which contains the index i.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each character in the string, we find the shortest unique substring that contains it.
If all characters in the string are the same, the shortest unique substring for each character is the character itself.
If all characters in the string are unique, the shortest unique substring for each character is the character itself.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over each character in the string and checking all possible substrings that contain the current character. For each substring, we count the number of occurrences in the string. If a substring occurs only once, we update the minimum length and starting position if necessary.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^3) per test case, O(N^3) total. The time complexity is cubic due to the nested loops for generating substrings and counting their occurrences.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the input string and the substrings generated during the process.
